question: What are the two key functions of 'Symbiotic Spores' in Xylotian terraforming pods?

answers.ground_truth: 'Symbiotic Spores' in Xylotian terraforming pods first break down hostile soil into a nutrient-rich substrate and then release Xylotian flora seeds for atmospheric oxygenation.
answers.original: Symbiotic Spores break down hostile soil and release Xylotian flora seeds to begin atmospheric oxygenation.
answers.perturbed: I don't have enough information to answer.

entities.name: Symbiotic Spores
entities.description: Symbiotic Spores are genetically engineered organisms tasked with breaking down hostile soil and releasing Xylotian flora seeds.

entities.name: Xylotian Terraforming Pods
entities.description: Xylotian terraforming pods are devices used to transform hostile exoplanet environments into habitable ones.

entities.name: Xylotian Years
entities.description: Xylotian years are the time units used to measure the duration of the terraforming process.

relations.description: The success rate of Xylotian terraforming efforts has been steadily increasing due to the use of Symbiotic Spores.<SEP>Each terraforming pod contains enough Symbiotic Spores to initiate a small, localized ecosystem.<SEP>Xylotian terraforming pods utilize Symbiotic Spores to break down hostile soil.
The process initiated by Symbiotic Spores can take several Xylotian years to show significant results.
Symbiotic Spores release Xylotian flora seeds to begin atmospheric oxygenation.
Xylotian terraforming pods are launched from Xylos towards potentially habitable exoplanets.
Deep-Space Telescopes help identify terraforming candidates for Symbiotic Spores to transform.
The outer shell of Xylotian terraforming pods is made from Impact-Resistant Ceramite.
